summary: Joel Spolsky posts a brief vacation notice announcing he'll be away for a couple of weeks. He teases a mystery icon discussion on the Fog Creek forums. He announces that CityDesk 2.0 will be released immediately upon his return. The bulk of the post highlights a pricing loophole where customers can buy the cheap 1.0 Home Edition and receive a free upgrade to the much more expensive 2.0 Pro Edition, saving $270.
key_points: CityDesk 2.0 is ready to ship and will be released as soon as Joel returns from vacation
A pricing loophole exists: buying CityDesk 1.0 Home for $79 gets you a free upgrade to 2.0 Pro, saving $270
The loophole is intentional — Fog Creek publicly promised free upgrades from any 1.0 edition to 2.0 Pro
money_quotes: There's a loophole the size of a truck going on right now with CityDesk pricing.
So basically while I'm looking at fjords and glaciers, you can buy the 1.0 home edition for $79 and get the 2.0 pro edition free when I get home, saving $270.
key_insight: Spolsky uses his vacation absence as a playful sales tactic, framing a generous upgrade promise as a time-limited pricing loophole to drive last-minute CityDesk 1.0 purchases before the 2.0 launch.
